mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the labor market signals impending economic contraction, causing investors to price in lower corporate profit growth and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive SPX returns in more than 60% of triggered instances, or the hazard ratio for negative returns drops below 1.2 in Cox proportional hazards testing.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in real GDP growth and housing starts indicates robust aggregate demand, which expands corporate revenue pipelines and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark by at least 150 basis points over any 3-year rolling period, or the interaction term coefficient in a multivariate regression becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply chain normalization, which lowers input cost inflation and supports equity valuations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in more than 55% of high INDPRO observations, or the rolling Spearman correlation between INDPRO month-over-month changes and 5-day SPX returns falls below 0.05 for two consecutive years.

mechanism_narrative: Increasing residential construction permits serve as a leading indicator of consumer confidence and future economic activity, directly boosting forward-looking equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The forward return differential between the top and bottom HOUST terciles is less than 50 basis points over a 20-day window, or the signal's information ratio drops below 0.2 over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index readings exceeding 225.0 indicate entrenched inflationary pressure, forcing the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy and compressing equity multiples, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns remain positive in more than 50% of periods where CPIAUCSL exceeds 225.0, or the predictive R-squared of a linear model using this threshold drops below 0.01 over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between strong payroll growth and a rising unemployment rate signals structural labor market imbalances that precede corporate margin compression, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The interaction term between PAYEMS growth and UNRATE changes fails to explain more than 2% of the variance in 20-day SPX returns, or the signal generates positive forward returns in over 65% of out-of-sample test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating growth in real GDP following a contraction phase reflects successful policy stimulus transmission, which rapidly restores corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day trading window.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following GDP acceleration episodes is statistically indistinguishable from zero (p > 0.05), or the signal's hit rate falls below 45% across three consecutive post-pandemic recovery cycles.
